title: L-Leucine based supramolecular gelator

Hydrochloric acid","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7hk8f","text":"Anhydrous magnesium sulphate","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5tnl","text":"Ethyl alcohol","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d3qtv","text":"Glucono delta lactone","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eak8e","text":"Tetrahydrofuran","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4eml2","text":"Acetonitrile","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"94i8","text":"Liquid paraffin\n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: L-Leucine (C6H13NO2) 2.62g (0.02 mol) and Cetyl alcohol (C16H34O) 4.85g (0.02mol) were dissolved in toluene (75 ml). P-toluene sulphonic acid monohydrate (C7H8O3S.H2O) 7.60g (0.04mol) was added.
The reaction mixture was refluxed at 95℃ for 10 hours.
The progress of the reaction was monitored by TLC (15% ethyl acetate in Hexane).
After completion of the reaction, toluene was removed using rotary evaporator and allowed to cool to room temperature.
The residue obtained was dissolved in dichloromethane (75ml) and washed with 10% sodium carbonate solution (70ml, twice), water (50ml, twice) and brine solution (50 ml).
The organic layer was dried over anhydrous magnesium sulphate. The dichloromethane was removed using rotary evaporator.
The amino ester obtained was purified by converting the amino ester hydrochloride salt by dissolving in acetone 100ml and Conc. Hydrochloric acid 25ml.

The solution was kept in refrigerator at 5℃ for 16 hours. The hydrochloride salt obtained was dissolved in DCM (75 ml) and washed with 10% sodium carbonate solution (50ml, twice), water (50ml, twice) and brine solution (50 ml). The organic layer was dried over anhydrous magnesium sulphate. Refluxing using Dean-Stark apparatus and reflux condenser The white solid obtained after toluene evaporation Washing using dichloromethane (75ml) followed by, 10% sodium carbonate solution (70ml, twice), water (50ml, twice) and brine solution. Formation of amino ester hydrochloride salt
The crude oily liquid obtained from first step (6.6g, 0.18 mmol) was dissolved in ethyl alcohol 75ml and Glucono delta lactone (C6H10O6) 3.6g (0.02mol) was added.
The mixture was refluxed for 8 hours.
The progress of the reaction was monitored by TLC.
After completion of the reaction, ethyl alcohol was removed by using rotary evaporator.
The crude compound was dissolved in THF and filtered hot.
The THF was removed to get colourless powder.

The powder obtained was further purified by recrystallisation in acetonitrile to get the colourless solid. Refluxing using reflux condenser Recrystallization in acetonitrile L-Leucine based supramolecular gelator

The dry gelator obtained after the process of recrystallization was weighed.The gelation properties were tested through the tube inversion test which, was done by dissolving the gelator using various solvents

The gelator was uniformly dissolved in liquid paraffin using a sonicator for 20 minutes at 60 ℃ which, which was very firm and stable even after 48 hours. Tube inversion test using various solvents %s %s Gelator Volume (mg) Solvent (ml) Inference 500 De-ionised water -1.5 ml Gel was partially formed at 30° C 500 NaCl (20mM) -1.5 ml Solvent is not completely miscible with the gelator 500 Acetone-1.5 ml Cracks are observed after the gel cools. 500 Toluene-1.5 ml Gel is formed after cooling (5 minutes) when kept in oil bath at 90° C and the gel remains stable even after 48 hours 500 Paraffin- 1 ml Gel completely solidifies after 20 minutes. Its stable even after 48 hours. There are no cracks 500 Ethanol-1.5 ml Slight cracks were observed after 24 hours.
